revision.text:
[[ja:第六天神社 (杉並区)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Tenjin Shrine |image = File:Dairokuten jinjya (Takaido-nishi, Suginami-ku, Tokyo) 05.JPG |Location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Suginami|en|Suginami|qid=Q232631|lt=Suginami Ward|1=Suginami}}{{ill|Takaido-nishi|ja|高井戸西|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaido-nishi|1=Takaido-Nishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11668701}}1-7-2 |Coordinates = {{wikicoord2|35|40|44.58|N|139|36|43.89|E|scale:25000|qq=}} |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|lt=Menashi no Mikoto and Kōne no Mikoto|lt_ja|面足命・惶根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omodaru and Ayakashikone|qq=}} |Shrine Status = Village Shrine (under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate Names = Kamitakaido Dairokuten Shrine
Dairokuten Shrine
Rokusho-sama
Dairokuten (name used before the Meiji era) |Annual Festival = {{ill|September 10|en|September 10|qid=Q2853|lt=September 10|1=September 10}} (see also [[#Festival Events]]) |Rituals = |deity = {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|qid=Q11152535|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}} |location = {{ill|Suginami-ku|qid=Q232631|1=Suginami}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.67905|N|139.61219167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Dairokuten Shrine''' (第六天神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|Suginami|en|Suginami|qid=Q232631|lt=Suginami Ward|1=Suginami}}{{ill|Takaido-nishi|ja|高井戸西|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaido-nishi|1=Takaido-Nishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11668701}}. According to the {{ill|Company register|en|Company register|qid=Q1394657|lt=Corporate Registration|1=Company register}}, the religious corporation is registered under the name '''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社).{{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place Names Dictionary 13: Tokyo|ja|角川日本地名大辞典13 東京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place Names Dictionary 13: Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by the Kadokawa Japan Place Names Editorial Committee, published by {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|qid=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1978, p.887 == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|lt=Menashi no Mikoto and Kōne no Mikoto|lt_ja|面足命・惶根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omodaru and Ayakashikone|qq=}} (おもだるのみこと・あやかしこねのみこと) == History == Although the date of its founding is unclear, it is said to date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, and in the {{ill|1559|en|1559|qid=Q6609|lt=1559|1=1559}} ({{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|qid=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 2) record of the {{ill|Odawara Retainer's Register|ja|小田原衆所領役帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odawara Retainer's Register|1=Odawara-shū Shoryō Yakuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11463001}}, it is mentioned as "Takaidōdō".[http://jinja.tokyolovers.jp/tokyo/suginami/takaido-dairokutenjinja {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine (Takaido-nishi)|ja|第六天神社 (高井戸西)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine (Takaido-nishi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] (Shrines and Goshuin) It is also said that this shrine was historically the guardian shrine of the area (former {{ill|Kamitakaido|ja|上高井戸|lt=Kamitakaido Village|lt_ja|上高井戸村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitakaido|1=Kami-Takaido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11360534}}) as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|qid=Q13479654|lt=Chinju-sha|1=Chinjusha}}.[http://www.tesshow.jp/suginami/shrine_takaidow_tenjin.html {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}}｜{{ill|Takaidō-nishi, Suginami|ja|杉並区高井戸西の神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaidō-nishi, Suginami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] (Neko no Ashiato) Furthermore, before the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era, this shrine was known as '''Dairokuten'''. During the {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} era ({{ill|1830|en|1830|qid=Q7576|lt=1830|1=1830}}–{{ill|1843|en|1843|qid=Q7629|lt=1843|1=1843}}), the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|神社建築#本殿|lt=Main Hall|lt_ja|本殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Hall|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080012}}) and the worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|神社建築#拝殿|lt=Worship Hall|lt_ja|拝殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worship Hall|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080012}}) were destroyed by fire. The shrine remained in a temporary state for a while, but the current main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1856|en|1856|qid=Q7676|lt=1856|1=1856}} ({{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|qid=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 3), and in November {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} ({{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}) it was classified as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System. Furthermore, the worship hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1881|en|1881|qid=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} ({{ill|1881|en|1881|qid=Q7808|lt=Meiji 14|1=1881}}). With the construction of the {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|qid=Q1091276|lt=Chūō Expressway|1=Chūō Expressway}}, the shrine buildings were relocated to the rear through {{ill|Structure relocation|en|Structure relocation|qid=Q826949|lt=hikeya (shrine relocation)|1=Structure relocation}}, and in June {{ill|1972|en|1972|qid=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 47) a new covered hall of the main hall and a new heiden ({{ill|Heiden (Offering Hall)|ja|神社建築#幣殿|lt=Heiden|lt_ja|幣殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiden (Offering Hall)|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080012}}) built in {{ill|Reinforced Concrete Construction|ja|鉄筋コンクリート構造|lt=Reinforced Concrete Building|lt_ja|鉄筋コンクリート造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reinforced Concrete Construction|1=reinforced concrete construction|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1577013}} were constructed.[http://www.shimotakaido.org/tenjinja/ {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website of {{ill|Shimotakaido Hamadayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|下高井戸浜田山八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotakaido Hamadayama Hachiman Shrine|1=Shimotakaido Hamadayama Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361507}}){{refnest|group="Notes"|According to “Shrines of Suginami”, the history states that the covered hall of the main hall and the heiden were newly constructed in June of Shōwa 47, whereas the Suginami Ward Board of Education’s shrine grounds sign "Tenjin Shrine" and the official website state that the reinforced concrete covered hall was completed in Shōwa 50.|qq=}}. In addition, at that time the roof of the worship hall was re-covered and a new subsidiary shrine building ({{ill|Subsidiary Shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subsidiary Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}}) enshrining the seven subsidiary deities on the grounds—constructed in reinforced concrete with a {{ill|Copper|en|Copper|qid=Q753|lt=Copper|1=Copper}} sheathed tiled roof—was built. Including many other Dairokuten Shrines (or Great Dairokuten Shrines), this shrine was originally established during the period of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu shūgō (Syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism)|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} to enshrine the {{ill|Sixth Heavenly Demon Hajun|ja|第六天魔王波旬|lt=Sixth Heavenly Demon Hajun|lt_ja|第六天魔王波旬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixth Heavenly Demon Hajun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hajun, Sanskrit: पापीयस् pāpīyas, also known as Tenma and Transcendent Deva); however, during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} period's {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja|オモダル・アヤカシコネ|lt=Menashi no Mikoto and Kōne no Mikoto|lt_ja|面足命・惶根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omodaru and Ayakashikone|qq=}}, the sixth in the divine lineage. This change originated from the heavenly ministry from which {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} himself adopted the name of the {{ill|Sixth Heavenly Demon|ja|第六天魔王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixth Heavenly Demon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天魔|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天魔|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天魔|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13578873}}. == Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds and the worship hall are open to visitors free of charge. On the grounds, the following subsidiary shrine buildings (seven shrines) are enshrined. Also, as one proceeds from the {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}} toward the worship hall, a pair of {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|qid=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} are placed. The front pair, built in {{ill|1917|en|1917|qid=Q2092|lt=1917|1=1917}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 6), and the rear pair, built in {{ill|1771|en|1771|qid=Q7686|lt=1771|1=1771}} ({{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|qid=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 8), are particularly valuable—the latter being older than the previous shrine building that burned down—and have been designated as a {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q858308|lt=Cultural Property|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} by Suginami Ward. [[File:Dairokuten jinjya (Takaido-nishi, Suginami-ku, Tokyo) 09.JPG|thumb|Torii at the shrine grounds entrance and the shrine sign]] === Subsidiary Shrines === * {{ill|Akiba Shrine|ja|秋葉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiba Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|qid=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen shrine}} – The Asama Shrine, formerly located in the Maezan area of the village, was relocated as a subsidiary shrine in {{ill|1897|en|1897|qid=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} (Meiji 30). * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|qid=Q11590310|lt=Tensho Shrine|1=Shinmei shrines}} – The Shinmei Shrine, formerly located in the Ise area of the village, was relocated as a subsidiary shrine in {{ill|1897|en|1897|qid=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} (Meiji 30). * {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|qid=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrine|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} * {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} (two shrines are enshrined) == Festivals == * {{ill|Annual Festival|ja|例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Festival|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11385469}}: {{ill|September 10|en|September 10|qid=Q2853|lt=September 10|1=September 10}} === Former Festivals === Before the Meiji period, the festival was held on {{ill|November 22|en|November 22|qid=Q3025|lt=November 22|1=November 22}}. Until around {{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), there was a custom ({{ill|Custom|ja|風習|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Custom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Social convention|qid=Q251777}}) known as "okomori," in which people, after finishing their agricultural work, would stay overnight in the worship hall while drinking sacred sake on the eve of the main festival. Additionally, in the early Shōwa period a rain-invoking {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}} was performed.From the shrine grounds sign "Tenjin Shrine" ({{ill|Board of education|en|Board of education|qid=Q399541|lt=Board of Education|1=Board of education}}, Shōwa 54 (1979) February 1) == Access == * Approximately 11 minutes on foot from the {{ill|Keio Inokashira Line|en|Keio Inokashira Line|qid=Q1191300|lt=Keio Inokashira Line|1=Keio Inokashira Line}} ''{{ill|Takaido Station|en|Takaido Station|qid=Q31309|lt=Takaido Station|1=Takaido Station}}'' == Gallery == File:Dairokuten jinjya (Takaido-nishi, Suginami-ku, Tokyo) 01.JPG|Torii at the shrine grounds entrance and the shrine sign File:Dairokuten jinjya (Takaido-nishi, Suginami-ku, Tokyo) 02.JPG|{{ill|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水}} File:Dairokuten jinjya (Takaido-nishi, Suginami-ku, Tokyo) 03.JPG|View of the worship hall from in front of the first pair of komainu File:Dairokuten jinjya (Takaido-nishi, Suginami-ku, Tokyo) 07.JPG|Worship hall and main hall from the left side == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help|qq=}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"|qq=}} === Sources === {{Reflist|qq=}} == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Shimotakaido Hamadayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|下高井戸浜田山八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotakaido Hamadayama Hachiman Shrine|1=Shimotakaido Hamadayama Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361507}} – Head Shrine * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Suginami)|ja|春日神社 (杉並区)|lt=Omiya-mae Kasuga Shrine|lt_ja|大宮前春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Suginami)|1=Kasuga Shrine (Suginami)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513668}} * {{ill|Kugayama Inari Shrine|ja|久我山稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugayama Inari Shrine|1=Kugayama Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369362}} * {{ill|Nishitakaido Shoan Inari Shrine|ja|西高井戸松庵稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitakaido Shoan Inari Shrine|1=Nishitakaido Shōan Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630006}} === Related Topics === {{commonscat|Dairokuten Shrine (Takaido-nishi, Suginami-ku, Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} – Exists in various locations, mainly in the {{ill|Kantō Region|ja|関東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Region|1=Kantō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11655841}}. == External Links == * [http://www.shimotakaido.org/tenjinja/ {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}}] (Official website of {{ill|Shimotakaido Hamadayama Hachiman Shrine|ja|下高井戸浜田山八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotakaido Hamadayama Hachiman Shrine|1=Shimotakaido Hamadayama Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361507}}) {{Shinto stub|qq=}} {{Dairokuten Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|第六天神社 (杉並区)|version=104649114|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:第六天神社 (杉並区)]] [[ja:第六天神社]] {{Commons category|Dairokuten jinjya (Takaido-nishi, Suginami-ku, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11602401}} [[Category:Dairokuten Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
